name: Salmon with Chantrelles
recipeIngredient: 24 oz Salmon Fillets or Steaks, cut 3/4 to 1 inch thick
Chopped fresh chervil, chives, and parsley
recipeInstructions:
Cooked Mushrooms:
1. In a large skillet, melt butter over medium heat.
2. Add chantrelles. Cover with a buttered round of waxed paper and lid.
3. Cook 5 minutes until tender. Remove chantrelles with a slotted spoon, reserving pan juices. Set aside.
Poached Salmon:
4. Add salmon, stock, wine, and shallots to the reserved pan juices in the skillet.
5. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat to medium-low.
6. Cover and simmer 10-12 minutes until fish flakes easily at the thickest part when tested with a fork.
7. Using two spatulas, carefully turn fish over halfway through cooking time.
8. Remove salmon with a slotted spatula, reserving pan juices. Cover salmon and keep warm.
Chantrelle Sauce:
9. Boil pan juices gently until reduced by half. Strain pan juices and return to skillet.
10. Stir in lemon juice, mustard, and cream. Add chantrelles.
11. Boil gently until thickened slightly. Season with salt and pepper.
12. Ladle sauce over salmon on serving plates. Sprinkle with herbs to garnish.
